title: Trump celebrates America’s 250th anniversary in weather-delayed, sometimes partisan speech
summary: President Trump capped off the 250th anniversary of the nation’s founding with a late-night speech delayed by inclement weather that blended the championing of American achievements and resilience with deeply partisan rhetoric about the perceived ideological threats facing the country. “Tonight we come together for one of the most joyous and glorious milestones of all time,”...

title: In Fourth of July Speech, Trump Celebrates America and Derides Foes
summary: A centerpiece address for the nation’s 250th anniversary celebration devoted time to American history, but had all the hallmarks of a Trump rally.

title: The Brooklyn Bridge Briefly Catches Fire During a Fireworks Display
summary: The fire appeared to have been extinguished by shortly after 10 p.m., the police said. No injuries were reported.

title: U.S. Ambassador to Canada Hosts July 4 Party Amid Tension Over New Bridge
summary: Pete Hoekstra had made false claims that Canada hadn’t fully paid for the $4.5 billion Gordie Howe Bridge between Detroit and Windsor, Ontario.

title: This California Town Banned Fireworks. Then Came the Dogs.
summary: People flock to the coastal town of Cambria, which has become known for its fireworks ban, to give their dogs some respite on the Fourth of July.

title: In Mount Rushmore speech, Trump veers from U.S. exceptionalism to warnings about communism
summary: President Trump ushered in America's 250th anniversary with a darkly political speech that swerved from the typically apolitical, unifying speeches past presidents have given to mark Independence Day.

title: 'Cautiously optimistic': Swing voters describe their view of America at 250
summary: As America turns 250, voters from our Swing Shift project talk about the state of the country. Their views ranged from "uncertain" and "concerned" to "excited" and "cautiously optimistic."

summary: While the language was similar to several other speeches Trump has given in recent days, it was notable for being delivered in a national park that commemorates some of America's most prominent presidents.

title: The Atlantic republishes JD Vance’s Trump op-ed from 2016

title: A Year Later, Trump’s Big Beautiful Bill Act Has a Clean Tech Silver Lining
summary: Trump’s One Big Beautiful Bill Act gutted the wind and solar credits at the heart of Biden’s Inflation Reduction Act. But a year on, the law’s preserved incentives for geothermal, advanced nuclear, and grid-scale storage may do more to cut global emissions than the credits it eliminated.

summary: Georgia's votes in that year's presidential race were counted three times, including once by hand, and each count affirmed Democrat Joe Biden's win.

title: Marriage Penalties and Bonuses in the Federal Tax Code

title: Farm Bill Primer: Programs Without a Budget Baseline
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) provides budgetary projections of government spending called baselines. title: How Schools and After-School Programs Can Collaborate to Support Academic Alignment
summary: The authors provide national data about the state of academic alignment between school-day and after-school learning, illustrate how schools and after-school programs put academic alignment into practice, and identify enabling and impeding factors.

title: Military Effectiveness Should Be Determined by The War Department Not Judges
summary: The military isn't a therapeutic institution; its purpose is to deter war, fight war, and win war. There are hard truths in military life that a humane society may dislike but cannot repeal. title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.
